story Print Main / ConstructorFunctionWithAnExplicitPrototype Here is a simple demonstration of use of constructors with inheritance (this example works with the current infrastructure): exports. BaseAccessor = function ( ) { accessor. input ( 'foo'

Accessor = function ( ) { accessor. input ( 'foo' ) ; } exports. DerivedAccessor = function ( ) { accessor. input ( 'bar' ) ; } exports. DerivedAccessor . prototype = new exports. BaseAccessor ( ) ; exports. setup = function ( ) { var test = new expo

xports. setup = function ( ) { var test = new exports. DerivedAccessor ( ) ; } [$[Get Code]] BaseAccessor and DerivedAccessor could each be in their own CommonJS module, in which case we could use "requires" to import it. Alternatively, we could defi

quires" to import it. Alternatively, we could define a utility function so that the definition of the derived accessor would look like this: exports. DerivedAccessor = function ( ) { accessor. input ( 'bar' ) ; } inherits ( exports. DerivedAccessor ,

'bar' ) ; } inherits ( exports. DerivedAccessor , 'BaseAccessor' ) ; [$[Get Code]] The inherits function combines requires() and the setting of the prototype. A more elaborate example below, which also works in the current infrastructure, demonstrat

so works in the current infrastructure, demonstrates inheritance of functions and invocation of the prototype function: // Base class for all accessors, with temporary implementation // to bridge to the current design. Accessor = function ( ) { this

he current design. Accessor = function ( ) { this . input = function ( args ) { accessor. input ( args ) ; } } MyAccessor = function ( ) { this . input ( 'bar' ) ; this . initialize = function ( ) { console. log ( "MyAccessor initialize." ) ; } } MyA

onsole. log ( "MyAccessor initialize." ) ; } } MyAccessor. prototype = new Accessor ( ) ; DerivedAccessor = function ( ) { this . input ( 'foo' ) ; this . initialize = function ( ) { console. log ( "DerivedAccessor initialize." ) ; // Song and dance

erivedAccessor initialize." ) ; // Song and dance to invoke the prototype's initialize() function. DerivedAccessor. prototype . initialize . apply ( this ) ; } } DerivedAccessor. prototype = new MyAccessor ( ) ; // Temporary bridge to current accesso

essor ( ) ; // Temporary bridge to current accessor functionality. var test ; exports. setup = function ( ) { test = new DerivedAccessor ( ) ; } exports. initialize = function ( ) { test. initialize ( ) ; } [$[Get Code]] Back to Research and Open Is
